name: Microsoft 365
description: Outlook email, Calendar, Tasks, Planner via MCP tools
agentDocs:
## Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Calendar, Tasks)

You have MCP tools prefixed with `mcp__ms365__` for accessing the user's Microsoft 365 account. Use these when the user asks about email, calendar events, or tasks.

Available capabilities:
- *Email*: list, read, search, create drafts, move, delete messages and attachments. You cannot send, forward, or reply to emails.
- *Calendar*: list, read, create, update, delete events. Accept/decline/tentatively accept invitations. View calendar availability.
- *Tasks*: list, read, create, update, delete To Do tasks and Planner tasks.

**MS365 Tasks is the single to-do/reminder surface on this install.** Route ALL task/todo/reminder phrasings through `mcp__ms365__create-todo-task` into their default task list (named `Tasks`). This includes:
- "add X to my to-do list" / "add X to my task list" / "add a task" → create a MS365 todo.
- "remind me to X" / "remind me at Y to X" → create a MS365 todo with `dueDateTime` set and `reminderDateTime` set for the nudge time. MS To Do syncs to the user's iOS Reminders via their Exchange account and will fire a notification at the reminder time.
- Email-triage action items → MS365 todo in the `Tasks` list with the sidecar metadata convention described in `/email-triage`.

Do NOT route anywhere else for task/reminder intent. Apple Reminders is not installed on this system (and if the user reinstalls it, the routing may change but that's an install-time decision, not a per-request one).

Mail rules: you CAN create/update/delete inbox rules via `mcp__ms365__create-mail-rule`, `update-mail-rule`, `delete-mail-rule`, `list-mail-rules`. Use these when the user asks to auto-route recurring senders (newsletters, digests) to a folder — a server-side rule is free (no agent wake-up on each message) and dramatically cheaper than triaging those items through the LLM.

Not available: sending mail, mailbox settings, files/OneDrive, contacts, Teams chat.

auth.description: Opens browser for Microsoft OAuth consent
